title: caltha palustris f. cornuta
intro: Caltha palustris f. cornuta, also known as marsh marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The marsh marigold can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, fever, and other infections.
